keyFindings: Senate voted 51-48 to begin debate on SAVE America Act requiring documentary proof of citizenship to register and photo ID to vote; provisions would take effect immediately impacting 2026 midterms if passed (CNBC/Votebeat)

DOJ suing 29 states and D.C. over voter rolls including sensitive data; federal courts dismissed cases in California, Michigan, and Oregon but DOJ appealed all three with language critics say is designed to preemptively undermine midterm results (Stateline/Brennan Center)

31 restrictive voting laws enacted in 16 states in 2025 will be in effect for 2026 midterms; Trump called for 'nationalizing' elections and threatened to impose voter-ID and ban mail-in voting by executive order despite lacking constitutional authority (NPR/Votebeat)

Chief Judge Schiltz tallied 210 ICE violations across 143 cases during Operation Metro Surge; NJ judge found 17 separate injunction violations including migrants unlawfully transferred to Peru; criminal contempt proceedings threatened with potential jail time (Minnesota Reformer/NJ Monitor)

Over 100 DOJ prosecutors resigned citing political interference; Trump's personal attorney appointed as EDVA U.S. Attorney; judge dismissed retributive cases against Comey and Letitia James finding prosecutor was illegally appointed (Protect Democracy/IBA)

Judge dismissed DOJ subpoenas targeting Fed Chair Powell, reaffirming central bank independence; 150+ former federal and state judges signed bipartisan letter criticizing administration's broader attacks on the judiciary (Caracal Reports/CAP)

CISA acting associate director for cybersecurity resigned March 6; FY2026 budget eliminates Election Security Program entirely ($39.6M, 14 positions); workforce cut from 3,292 to 2,324 — a loss of nearly 1,000 employees (Federal News Network/Defense One)

Defense Secretary Hegseth ordered 20% reduction in four-star generals and 10% cut in all general/flag officers; retired Gen. McChrystal warned purges threaten 'irreversible damage' to military readiness (PBS/Seattle Times)

DOGE engineer allegedly exfiltrated SSA data on thumb drive with plans to share with private employer; Senate Intelligence Committee warned of 'enormous counterintelligence and security risks' from DOGE access to classified materials at seven agencies (Washington Post/NPR)

Schedule F/Schedule Policy-Career took effect March 8-9; up to 50,000 federal employees in policy-influencing roles face reclassification into at-will positions; employee unions revived lawsuit to block implementation (Federal News Network/Government Executive)

D.C. Circuit ruled 2-1 that NLRB/MSPB firings were lawful, emboldening further action; 56+ political appointees fired across independent agencies; CFPB en banc hearing on whether executive branch can effectively disable a congressionally-created agency (CREW/NPR)

'10-for-1' deregulation order driving sweeping rollbacks; SEC extended compliance deadlines and reduced reporting requirements; new March 2026 EO targets housing regulatory barriers (Federal Register/Lexology)

Court of International Trade ordered CBP to refund all IEEPA tariffs nationwide — approximately $166B across 20.1M unliquidated entries; government's stay request denied, appeal to Federal Circuit expected (Holland & Knight/Sullivan & Cromwell)

Section 122 tariffs at 10.5% effective rate, highest since 1943; 24 states filed legal challenge in Court of International Trade; statute has hard 150-day limit expiring July 24 (Yale Budget Lab/Cherry Bekaert)

J.P. Morgan places recession probability at 40-50% over next 12 months; EU planning $108B in retaliatory tariffs; average US household faces $1,500 tariff-driven tax increase; GDP forecasts cut by 0.9 percentage points for 2026 (J.P. Morgan/Tax Foundation)

DOGE staffer Nate Cavanaugh admitted under oath that DOGE failed to reduce the federal deficit; original $2T savings target walked back to $200B in 'zombie payments' while disruption estimated to cost taxpayers $135B (Fortune/WBUR)

DOGE pressured FAA administrator to resign, directed firing of safety-critical employees; FAA began canceling $2.4B Verizon contract while simultaneously adopting Musk's Starlink for air traffic control; Musk holds $15B+ in government contracts (Campaign Legal Center/CBS News)

Treasury cancelled all 31 contracts with Booz Allen Hamilton ($21M) — procurement experts called it unprecedented; DOJ dropped defense of executive orders targeting law firms that represented Trump's opponents (Federal News Network/CBS News)

DHS partial shutdown entering 5th week with 260,000+ personnel affected; TSA officers missing full paychecks during peak spring break travel; no resolution in sight as House-passed H.R. 7744 faces Senate roadblocks (Washington Post/CNBC)

Senator Tillis joined Democrats blocking all Fed confirmations until DOJ resolves Judge Boasberg ruling; administration floated requiring Fed Chair to consult president on rate decisions; Warsh nomination stalled (CNBC/Financial Content)

Deficit hit $1T in first 5 months of FY2026, on track for $1.9T annual (5.8% of GDP); debt at 101% of GDP projected to reach 120% by 2036; potential $166B IEEPA tariff refund further destabilizes fiscal outlook (CBO/CRFB)

V-Dem Institute 2026 Democracy Report found US freedom of expression 'at lowest level since end of WWII'; Liberal Democracy Index score fell 24% in one year, global ranking dropped from 20th to 51st (CNN/Al Jazeera)

Nashville Noticias reporter Estefany Rodriguez arrested by ICE March 4 despite legal status and open asylum case; attorneys allege retaliation for critical reporting; 41 press freedom organizations called for release (CPJ/ABC News)

Approximately $3.3B in federal research funding cut from ~90 universities; UCLA received settlement demanding restriction of campus speech and $1.2B payment; Texas A&M prohibited teaching portions of Plato touching on 'race and gender ideology' (Washington Post/Inside Higher Ed)

U.S. Marshals recorded 241 threats against judges in 2026 through mid-March, on pace to nearly double 2025's 564; organized pizza intimidation campaign used murdered Judge Salas' son's name; a congressman posted 'wanted poster' of federal judges (Washington Times/CBS News)

At least 33 pardoned Jan 6 defendants charged with subsequent crimes; pardoned rioter Jake Lang threatened officer should be 'put down like a dead dog'; pardoned defendants publicly called for prosecution of judges and prosecutors who oversaw their cases (CREW/NPR)

FBI investigating 2,700 domestic terrorism cases, up from 850 three years ago; Capitol Police report 7,500 active threats against elected officials; women in local office report 83% harassment rate suppressing civic participation (CSIS/PBS)

Schedule F/Policy-Career effective March 8-9, reclassifying up to 50,000 career positions as at-will with no MSPB appeal rights, pay incentives, or student loan benefits (Federal News Network/Government Executive)

Federal government shed 300,000+ employees (13.7% of Sept 2024 levels); OPM proposed rule to streamline future layoffs emphasizing performance over seniority; OMB states further cuts are 'priority number one' (Washington Post/CBPP)

SSA inspector general investigating DOGE staff data misuse including alleged attempt to copy NUMIDENT database (SSNs for nearly every living American) to private cloud; 3,000+ government datasets removed; National Press Club condemned disappearance of federal data during Sunshine Week (NPR/Marketplace)
Texas anti-ESG boycott law (SB 13) struck down as unconstitutional under First and Fourteenth Amendments; ruling could cascade to similar legislation in dozens of red states (Harvard Law/Freshfields)

59% of S&P 500 companies revised or removed DEI-related disclosures from SEC filings since January 2025; boycott campaigns intensifying from both progressive and conservative groups targeting Home Depot, Spotify, Tesla, and others (U.S. News/A&O Shearman)

DOJ abandoned defense of executive orders targeting law firms that represented Trump opponents; broader chilling effect on corporate political speech and legal advocacy already embedded despite legal retreat (CBS News/Washington Post)
